description: Implement DAG construction from tasks and scheduling logic in internal/dag/. Build a directed acyclic graph from task prerequisites, detect cycles (Kahn's algorithm or DFS), compute readiness based on lifecycle statuses, and determine execution waves. Reference docs/implementation-plan.md Milestone 3 for the DAG struct, scheduler, and all method signatures.
commit: feat(dag): add DAG construction with cycle detection and readiness scheduling
steps: Create internal/dag/dag.go with the DAG struct (nodes, forward edges, reverse edges). Implement Build(tasks []task.Task) (*DAG, error) with cycle detection and missing-prerequisite validation. Implement Prerequisites(), Dependents(), Roots().

Create internal/dag/scheduler.go with ReadinessChecker. Implement IsReady(taskID, statuses map) that returns true when all prerequisites are merged. Implement ReadyTasks(statuses) returning pending tasks with all-merged prerequisites sorted by priority. Implement ComputeWaves(statuses) for execution wave preview.

Create internal/dag/dag_test.go: test valid DAG construction, test cycle detection (A->B->C->A), test missing prerequisite error, test IsReady with various status maps, test ReadyTasks returns correct priority ordering, test ComputeWaves with known graph
acceptance_criteria: Build detects cycles and returns descriptive error including the cycle path
Build detects missing prerequisites and returns error with the missing ID
Prerequisites and Dependents return correct adjacency lists
Roots returns tasks with no prerequisites
IsReady returns true only when ALL prerequisites have status merged
ReadyTasks returns tasks sorted by priority (lower number = higher priority)
ComputeWaves correctly assigns tasks to theoretical execution waves
